piece.line: Because you were on the brink of something beautiful, but you couldn’t quite reach it and you fell too far
piece.author: Bianca Phipps
piece.title: Almosts

piece.line: I was carved hallow by loving hands hold wars in the spaces between my ribs
piece.author: Bianca Phipps
piece.title: Born To Embody It

piece.line: You cannot break a mangled thing and you don’t know the last time your heart sounded like a heart
piece.author: Bianca Phipps
piece.title: The Heartbreaker piece

piece.line: When I sound like this I know my loved ones can find me
piece.author: Erin Schick
piece.title: Honest Speech

piece.line: When we are gone, what tangible life will be left?
piece.author: Ken Arkind
piece.title: David

piece.line: We all learned it from each other, the way each generation taught the next how to knit, weaving silence in between the threads
piece.author: Lily Myers
piece.title: shrinking Women

piece.line: We build bridges, we write books and hold grudges
piece.author: Meghann Plunkett
piece.title: Humans

piece.line: My heart would rattle and shake with all of the coins spilling back into it. I would stop digging around in my pockets for pieces of myself
piece.author: Melissa Lozada-Olivia
piece.title: If I Got Paid For All My Emotional Labor

piece.line: Ma mère n'a jamais eu des ailes
piece.author: Pages Matam
piece.title: Wings

piece.line: Know that your legs were made to run, your bones were made to heal
piece.author: Phil Kaye
piece.title: Beginning, Middle and End

piece.line: You make the same mistake over and over; you'll stop calling it a mistake
piece.author: Phil Kaye
piece.title: Repetition

piece.line: Strength from never being enough; healing from an open wound
piece.author: Samira Obeid
piece.title: Pigtails

piece.line: Because no matter how wide you stretch your fingers, your hands will always be too small to catch all the pain you want to heal.
piece.author: Sarah Kay
piece.title: If I Should Have A Daughter
